# slm-coding-bench report — `m4-roster-v1`

Samples per task: **1** (greedy pass@1).

> **Caveat:** `pass@1 (bench)` covers HumanEval/MBPP-style tasks that are likely present in model training data — treat it as comparability, not capability. The `custom` columns and the parity/perf suites are the contamination-resistant signal.

## Quality

| Model | Deploy | Solver | pass@1 (all) | pass@1 (custom) | pass@1 (bench) | parity | perf ratio | extract | tasks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| mlx-community/Qwen2.5-Coder-3B-Instruct-4bit | mlx_m4 | single_agent | 87.5% | 75.0% | 100.0% | 93.9% | 1.32× | 100.0% | 16 |
| mlx-community/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct-4bit | mlx_m4 | single_agent | 100.0% | 100.0% | 100.0% | 100.0% | 1.29× | 100.0% | 16 |
| mlx-community/Qwen3-1.7B-4bit | mlx_m4 | single_agent | 31.2% | 25.0% | 37.5% | 28.6% | 0.97× | 100.0% | 16 |

_perf ratio = candidate runtime / reference runtime (geomean; lower is faster, advisory). parity = mean differential-agreement rate vs reference._
